pattern: Two-pointer run-length encoding writes the character then its count digit-by-digit into the same array, advancing a write pointer that never overtakes the read pointer.
hints: Scan with a read pointer, identify each run of consecutive identical characters and its length before writing anything.
Write the character at the write position; if the run length is greater than 1, convert the count to its individual digit characters and write each digit separately.
Return the final value of the write pointer — it equals the new length of the compressed prefix.

edge_inputs.description: mixed runs: aa bb ccc compresses to a2b2c3, length 6
single character run — no count appended, length stays 1
run of 12 b's produces b12, length 4 (count spans two digits)
run of exactly 10 — count is two digits; naive single-digit check fails
run of 100 — three-digit count must be split into three separate characters
all distinct characters — nothing compresses, length equals input length
two-character array, both same — compresses to char+2, length 2
mixed single and group: one a, three b's, one c

stress.description: long array of random lowercase letters producing many runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    chars = []
    while len(chars) < size:
        c = rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase)
        run = rng.randint(1, min(20, size - len(chars)))
        chars.extend([c] * run)
    chars = chars[:size]
    return [chars]
